jobcontent:

jobcontent:
Our sunny team is looking

SERVICE MANAGER FOR FLOOR MAINTENANCE TASKS IN OULU

We are looking for a hotel cleaner for a permanent part-time job at a hotel in Oulu. In this position, you get to work on versatile hotel cleaning tasks and act as an interactive supervisor. The task includes:

- Floor cleaning including cleaning of hotel rooms, replenishment of supplies and reporting to the team on the required measures.

- Guiding, shift planning and ensuring equal distribution of work tasks.

- Making high-quality cooperation with other hotel employees to ensure the quality of the visitors' service experience.

SOL's hotel cleaning has the Joutsenmarkki and we follow the standards according to its criteria in all hotel cleaning activities. So you get to participate in environmentally friendly cleaning activities and you will receive an introduction to cleaning work according to the criteria of the Joutsenmarki.

The work starts as soon as a suitable person is found. You get to jump right into the middle of our hotel's busiest season, where your initiative and ability to take action are valued. This is a great opportunity to quickly get into the ways of the house and develop your professional skills in a dynamic environment.

What do we expect from you?

- Previous experience in hotel cleaning, experience in the duties of a supervisor is considered an advantage.

- Good communication skills, as you will be dealing with both the team and customers.

- You communicate fluently in English in our multilingual work environment and in Finnish with reception and maintenance.

- The position requires an adult.

What do we offer you?

You will become part of a versatile and internationally operating Finnish family business. We invest in the well-being and occupational safety of our employees through the SOL Life program. We also offer:

- Permanent part-time job min. 30 hours/week, additional hours are possible if needed.

- We ensure a thorough introduction to your job duties and the cleaning practices according to Joutsenmerki.

- Your supervisor and the entire team will actively support you.

- Opportunities to train and advance in your career with the help of various career paths.

Salary complies with the collective agreement for employees in the Real Estate Service Industry (PR 23, i.e. €12.2612.88/h) depending on the tasks. The work tasks are mainly in salary group 3 (€12.88/h), and in addition, the duties of the shift supervisor are paid an RV supplement (€1.04/h).

The working hours are from Monday to Sunday between 0716.

Were you interested? Great! If you feel that you are a sunny and responsible team player who enjoys a busy hotel environment, please submit your application as soon as possible. We invite applicants to interviews already within the application period. For more information about the position, please contact Anna Niemikorpi, first name.surname (at) sol.fi. Please note that we do not accept applications by e-mail.

We offer

- Exercise and massage benefit

- Occupational health care that is wider than the statutory provisions

- Employee benefits valid after the trial period.

SOL Palvelut Oy

Having fun at work and a supportive atmosphere are important to us. Our operations are guided by the SOL values: sunny satisfied customer, everyday creativity, entrepreneurship, reliability and joyful work.
If these values ​​resonate with you, you might be the person we're looking for!
Self-initiative, responsibility and punctuality are also qualities we value.

Shift manager for hotel cleaning

Our sunny team is looking

SERVICE MANAGER FOR FLOOR MAINTENANCE TASKS IN OULU

We are looking for a hotel cleaner for a permanent part-time job at a hotel in Oulu. In this position, you get to work on versatile hotel cleaning tasks and act as an interactive supervisor. The task includes:

- Floor cleaning including cleaning of hotel rooms, replenishment of supplies and reporting to the team on the required measures.

- Guiding, shift planning and ensuring equal distribution of work tasks.

- Making high-quality cooperation with other hotel employees to ensure the quality of the visitors' service experience.

SOL's hotel cleaning has the Joutsenmarkki and we follow the standards according to its criteria in all hotel cleaning activities. So you get to participate in environmentally friendly cleaning activities and you will receive an introduction to cleaning work according to the criteria of the Joutsenmarki.

The work starts as soon as a suitable person is found. You get to jump right into the middle of our hotel's busiest season, where your initiative and ability to take action are valued. This is a great opportunity to quickly get into the ways of the house and develop your professional skills in a dynamic environment.

What do we expect from you?

- Previous experience in hotel cleaning, experience in the duties of a supervisor is considered an advantage.

- Good communication skills, as you will be dealing with both the team and customers.

- You communicate fluently in English in our multilingual work environment and in Finnish with reception and maintenance.

- The position requires an adult.

What do we offer you?

You will become part of a versatile and internationally operating Finnish family business. We invest in the well-being and occupational safety of our employees through the SOL Life program. We also offer:

- Permanent part-time job min. 30 hours/week, additional hours are possible if needed.

- We ensure a thorough introduction to your job duties and the cleaning practices according to Joutsenmerki.

- Your supervisor and the entire team will actively support you.

- Opportunities to train and advance in your career with the help of various career paths.

Salary complies with the collective agreement for employees in the Real Estate Service Industry (PR 23, i.e. €12.2612.88/h) depending on the tasks. The work tasks are mainly in salary group 3 (€12.88/h), and in addition, the duties of the shift supervisor are paid an RV supplement (€1.04/h).

The working hours are from Monday to Sunday between 0716.

Were you interested? Great! If you feel that you are a sunny and responsible team player who enjoys a busy hotel environment, please submit your application as soon as possible. jobcontent:
We are supplementing the talent bank and looking for our client

MULTI-SERVICE WORKERS / CLEANERS

for gigs and substitutes of different lengths to work in schools and kindergartens in the Nurmijärvi area.

The duties of a multi-service employee include maintenance cleaning and periodic basic cleaning tasks in schools and daycares, as well as dish maintenance, line filling and other kitchen support tasks in addition to cleaning work in the kitchen.

The work takes place in the Nurmijärvi area and it is a gig job based on our client's continuous work shift needs. Shifts can be offered at short notice or after a longer period of time, the lengths of the shifts vary between 48 hours at the locations and the shifts are focused on weekdays. Despite the gig nature of the work, work can be offered continuously for longer periods of time.

The person selected for the position must have a hygiene passport, and must present a criminal background check for working with children before starting work and be able to get along at work in Finnish in order to understand the instructions. A driver's license and the possibility to use your own car make it easier to get to the destinations.

The employment relationship is concluded between StaffPoint and the employee. Salary at work in accordance with KVTES.

We require you

- Education and/or work experience in similar tasks, as well as a brisk attitude and a desire to do cleaning work and kitchen assistant tasks

- Hygiene passport

- Criminal records from all those working with children under the age of 18.

- Self-initiative and the ability to work independently

- Oral and written Finnish language skills to understand the instructions

We offer you

- Versatile tasks in different locations

- Valuable work experience and nice colleagues

- Opportunity for long-term work

Were you interested? Leave your application via the attached link. Unfortunately, we cannot process applications received by e-mail at all.

jobcontent:
We are looking for a brisk and reliable person to join our sunny group

SUMMER EMPLOYEE FOR HOTEL CLEANING IN RAUMA

for a temporary, part-time employment relationship.

The work mostly includes independent cleaning in a small hotel location, but to some extent you also get to do hours in other locations in the area when the team needs your help.

At the hotel, work tasks include e.g.

- Cleaning and preparing the rooms: Making the beds and changing the linen, emptying the trash, wiping dust and cleaning the surfaces, vacuuming and mopping the floors, and cleaning the bathroom: toilet seat and facilities, sink, shower or bathtub, and mirrors.

- Cleaning of common areas: Cleaning of lobby and reception areas and vacuuming and cleaning of corridors and staircases.

- Teamwork and communication with the accommodation services staff.

- Inspection and reporting: Reporting of damages and maintenance needs to the supervisor.

- Management of cleaning tools and materials: Maintenance of cleaning tools and replenishment of cleaning materials.

The job of a hotel cleaner is an important part of the accommodation facility's operation, as cleanliness and comfort are key to the visitors' experience.

The work is physically demanding at times. The corridors are narrow, the piles of linen are heavy, and the temperature in the summer months may rise to heat the interior as well. This job is just right for you if you want an active summer job, during which the day's training will be completed while you earn money.

Working hours: Shifts take place between Monday and Saturday, starting at 9:00 a.m. Hours per week are accumulated 5 hours/week according to the work shift list.

Work begins: As soon as a suitable person is found

Work ends: 28 August 2026

We expect the applicant to:

- Coming of age

- Good knowledge of Finnish or English.

- Good physical condition.

- Sunny customer service attitude and willingness to commit to work tasks.

- An independent approach to work

We consider education and/or experience in the field to be an advantage, but in any case we will familiarize you carefully with your tasks.

We offer

- An active summer job where you can do your work independently.

- Good and professional tools to do your work.

- Continuous support from your supervisor for the duration of your employment.

Salary follows the collective agreement of the real estate service industry in salary group 2 (€12.26/h).

Were you interested? Please apply for a position as soon as possible by filling out our electronic application form carefully and attaching your resume and/or application letter. We start reviewing applications already during the application period.

For more information about the position, please contact Jade Sivonen, firstname.surname(at)sol.fi, tel. 050 473 3658.

jobcontent:
Data architect for permanent employment
 
We are looking for an experienced Data Architect for our client for a permanent employment relationship. In the position, you get to work on modern data projects, especially with clients in the industrial sector. Snowflake, Databricks and other modern data platforms are used in the projects.
Your place of work can be at the office in Tampere, Helsinki or Turku. It is possible to do the work mostly remotely, however, taking into account customer needs and projects.
 
About the role:
- As a data architect, you play a central expert role in customer projects and are responsible for planning, developing and building the overall architecture of data solutions.
- You get to work on business-critical data projects and help customers make better use of data in decision-making, processes and business development.
- You currently have an expert team of around five people around you, which is planned to be increased in the near future. In this role, you also have the opportunity to influence how the team is built and what kind of expertise is developed around it.
 
What you can do:

In your assignment you can, for example:
- To design and develop modern data architectures for customer projects
- To work with Snowflake, Databricks or similar data platforms
- To participate in the development of industrial customers' data solutions
- To act as a technical advisor and solution designer for customers
- To support the development of the team's skills and operating models
- To influence how the growing data team is built in the future

What we expect from you:
 We hope that you have previous experience with data architecture and the ability to understand broad technical entities from the customer's business perspective.

We especially value:
- Experience in the tasks of a data architect or a similar expert role
- Knowledge of Snowflake, Databricks or similar modern data platforms
- Customer-oriented and solution-oriented way of working
- Ability to discuss technical solutions with both customers and the team
- Want to develop your own skills and also participate in team development
- Fluent Finnish language skills

What we offer you:
- On offer is a permanent role in a growing team of data experts, where you can influence both customer solutions and the future direction of the team.

jobcontent:
Are you interested in web programming or working with IT infrastructure? Employers in the Uusimaa region are now looking for AI experts, programming developers and IT experts for various roles!
Opiframe, together with partner companies, trains new experts
Full Stack & System Developer Recruitment Training
with the help of The training is suitable for both juniors and professionals. After the program, depending on your starting level, you will have the skills to work in versatile tasks in the ICT sector. The persons participating in the training can be oriented either
for web programming
or
to system services
(e.g. ICT specialist, IT support and cloud services).
Our partner companies have positions in a wide variety of IT-related positions, where you can utilize your skills. These include e.g. artificial intelligence expertise, web programming, ICT specialist and system services, content production, testing, integrations/architecture, DevOps environments and other operational tasks (e.g. Project Manager, Team Lead).
This is a good opportunity for you to find the job of your dreams, because the previous Full Stack & System Developer Recruitment Training employed more than 80% of the trainees. So feel free to apply!
The training will take place in Espoo from 4 June to 26 November 2026! The application ends on April 30, 2026.
Read more about the training and apply immediately:
https://opiframe.com/full-stack-jarjestelkehittajan-rekry-uusimaa-kevat-2026/
Description and content of the training program
The training offers a comprehensive overview of modern
of software development
as well as
system and cloud service environments
. Participants can deepen their expertise in two complementary areas of expertise: web development and companies' IT infrastructure and system environments. Competence can be emphasized in two different directions, depending on the needs of the cooperating company and the participant's background.
1. Web programming
The trend focuses on modern web development and practical work in a business environment. The program covers the operating principles of web programming and the use of artificial intelligence in development. Key topics, for example:
Front end:
HTML, CSS, Java/TypeScript, React, Angular, RESTful APIs
Back end:
Implementation of NodeJS, Express, Java / Spring Boot, RESTful API interfaces
Databases:
SQL (PostgreSQL, MySQL), NoSQL (MongoDB), data modeling and data processing
2. System and cloud services
The orientation focuses on the company's infrastructure and support functions, including cloud-managed IT infrastructure, Windows servers, backups and information security. We also go through Microsoft 365 environments such as Microsoft Copilot, M365 and Active Directory.
Artificial intelligence and its utilization
The training also introduces the use of artificial intelligence.
AI-assisted programming
utilization of development tools
the use of artificial intelligence in the company's support functions
The training is implemented as a multi-modal education, where learning mainly takes place through on-the-job learning (approx. 80 days) in a cooperative company. In addition to on-the-job learning, the program includes approx. 40–45 days of knowledge-based training, which consists of face-to-face teaching days in Espoo as well as online training and guided self-study.
Target group
In the training, you learn modern software development in a practical way and get to deepen your knowledge of either software development or system environments.
The training is suitable for people with experience in software development or technical system environments.
For example, we require applicants to have one of the following backgrounds:
applicable work experience in the ICT sector
university degree (e.g. information technology, software technology, automation, electronics)
Verifiable programming experience with, for example, JavaScript, Python, Java, C# or other languages
Companies especially value an interest in technologies and system architecture, a desire to constantly learn new things, problem-solving skills and the ability to work in a development team.
The training is workforce training, which is primarily aimed at unemployed and job seekers at risk of unemployment in the Uusimaa employment area. The teaching is organized in Finnish, so the participants are required to have fluent oral and written Finnish language skills and the readiness to understand study material in English.
Application instructions and schedule
1.
Apply for training through Työmarkkinatori
no later than 30.4.
(Education number 26058230). Pre-selection for the training is done based on the application, so in your application you should highlight your skills and motivation through practical examples, for example in descriptions of previous work assignments or your own projects.
2. In addition, send your resume to Opiframe by e-mail to the address
fullstack@opiframe.com
. Name your CV in the format "Surname First Name CV".
You should apply for the training as soon as possible, because places are already filled during the application period!
If you have any questions about the training, feel free to contact:
Otto Kiiskinen /
otto.kiiskinen@opiframe.com
/ +358 40 0164 450
Toivo Osei /
hope

.osei@opiframe.com
/ +358 40 758 2591
You can find more information about the training on Opiframe's website:
https://opiframe.com/full-stack-jarjestelkehittajan-rekry-uusimaa-kevat-2026/
Our training is organized in cooperation with Uusimaa's employment regions.

title: Disc processing center and laser programmer (unloader)
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
what_we_expect: You have previous experience in similar tasks and a good understanding of technical documentation and drawings
Good knowledge of disc manufacturing technology, previous programming experience and disc work center / laser user experience is an advantage
Good Finnish and English language skills. Your English doesn't have to be perfect, but at least you use it boldly
Active, self-directed, systematic approach to work
Cooperation and a developing approach
The work is demanding and sensitive to change, programming is done according to the order backlog. Persistence and pressure tolerance are therefore important qualities in succeeding in the task.
what_we_offer: Everyday work in one shift
A significant role in a growing and stable company
A competent and supportive team around you
The opportunity to develop your skills at the heart of modern production
Salary according to your experience (Technology industry test)
Were you interested?
jobcontent:
Are you interested in working in a growing and developing contract manufacturing company in the metal sector working with versatile steel sheet components?

We are now looking for a plate machining center and laser programmer (engraver) for our work team to strengthen the critical front end of our production. In this role, you can directly influence production efficiency, material use and delivery reliability.

Job duties:

- You play an important role starting from the first stages of our production, determining the timely programming of our sheet machining centers and laser cutting machines, taking into account the best possible material use planning

- In your work, you will not only program our laser and combi machines for cutting, but also

demolition robot of the latest technology

- You will use NC-Express software in your work, (Trutops and JetCam knowledge is an advantage)

What do we expect from you?

- You have previous experience in similar tasks and a good understanding of technical documentation and drawings

- Good knowledge of disc manufacturing technology, previous programming experience and disc work center / laser user experience is an advantage

- Good knowledge of Finnish and English. Your English doesn't have to be perfect, but at least you use it boldly

- Active, self-directed, systematic approach to work

- Cooperation and developing approach

The work is demanding and sensitive to change, programming is done according to the order backlog. Persistence and pressure tolerance are therefore important qualities in succeeding in the task.

What do we offer you?

- Everyday work in one shift

- A significant role in a growing and stable company

- A competent and supportive team around you

- The opportunity to develop your skills at the heart of modern production

- Salary according to your experience (Technology industry test)

Were you interested?

Send your application and CV as soon as possible, no later than 24 May 2026. by. We are primarily looking for experts who have already gained experience, but we also consider potential factors and we are interested in e.g. the skills behind operator or machine operation. The work starts according to the agreement, preferably as soon as possible. Laura Rekrytointi helps with recruitment, but the employment relationship is made directly with Steelcomp Vaasa Oy. For more information about the position, please contact Veera Kähäri, veera.kahari@laura.fi

what_we_offer: Apply now!
If this opportunity feels like your next step, send us your application by

Think of Visma as an ecosystem of companies, cultures, and people, united by shared values and the vision to
shape the future of society through technology
. In Finland we already have 1450+ employees and serve more than 200 000 customers.
Visit us at
visma.fi
jobcontent:
We want you to shine!
Our accounting office partners manage the financial administration of over 50 000 Finnish companies and entrepreneurs by using
Fivaldi
. Our goal is to transform the industry by digitalizing the everyday financial management for small and medium-sized businesses.
We are now looking for an
AI Platform Engineer
to drive the development and adoption of AI-powered solutions for our product development organization.
What to expect when joining us?
This is a hands-on platform role at the intersection of developer tooling, AI/ML infrastructure, and software engineering. You'll be designing and building the internal platform, pipelines, and foundational components that let our product teams move faster, automate more, and ship with confidence. This role centers on empowering development teams to adopt new AI-driven development practices and create AI-powered features for end users.
You will design and build our internal AI platform: services, tooling, APIs, and agentic workflows that developers across the organization can build on.
You will drive the adoption of AI-assisted practices across the software development lifecycle, from idea to production.
You will integrate and use shared infrastructure and services for AI/ML workloads, keeping systems production-grade, observable, and cost-efficient.
You will build and maintain automation pipelines that integrate AI capabilities.
You will define platform standards: reliability, security, cost governance, and operational runbooks.
You will partner with developers, software architects, AI architects as well as other stakeholders to turn emerging AI capabilities into practical, reusable platform components.
You will facilitate technical working groups and team syncs to align direction and unblock teams.
Our product development organization consists of ~30 people. You'll belong in our Platform & Infrastructure team, but work in close collaboration with cross-functional product teams, translating their needs into reliable and scalable platform capabilities.
How to succeed?
You're a platform engineer who is motivated by making other engineers faster and more effective. You prototype quickly, care about production quality, and know how to take something from proof-of-concept to a platform other teams can rely on. You're comfortable driving things forward without waiting for perfect clarity.
Your background:
Several years in cloud-native software development, platform or infrastructure engineering, with production experience (familiarity with AWS is a plus).
Strong programming skills, with hands-on experience shipping work using agentic coding tools (e.g. Claude Code).
Experience with LLMOps/MLOps, familiarity with services like Amazon Bedrock, SageMaker, or equivalent.
Informed perspective on AI in product development, grounded in concrete work you can point to.
Experience in designing and maintaining developer platforms, agentic workflows, internal tooling, or shared services at scale.
Solid grasp of CI/CD, developer experience, and modern software delivery practices.
Collaboration skills and proven ability to drive change across the product development organization, communicating clearly with both technical peers and non-technical stakeholders (in English - Finnish is considered an asset).
BSc or MSc degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.
Some nice to haves:
Background in platform product thinking: treating internal developers as your customers.
Familiarity with financial management, regulated environments, or enterprise-grade compliance requirements.
We want you to grow with us
We are a committed, curious, and solution-oriented collective of team players. With us you'll have the autonomy and responsibility to drive our shared goals, backed by the mentorship of your leaders and the collective expertise of Visma's AI community. We provide opportunities for professional growth, learning new skills, and advancing your career. We have the opportunity to use 10% of our working hours to learn new things. With us, you'll undoubtedly build your own growth story. We want you to shine!
At Visma Solutions you'll have a cosy and relaxed work environment with around 500 colleagues, modern tools, and comprehensive
employee benefits
that support work-life balance. Visma Solutions Oy has been awarded the Hyvän mielen työpaikka® symbol as a recognition of commitment to strengthening the mental health of employees. The multinational corporation combined with the agile approach of Visma family companies creates a unique work environment and excellent career opportunities.
Apply now!
If this opportunity feels like your next step, send us your application by
May 17th, 2026.
Visma Solutions offices are in Helsinki and Lappeenranta, but you can also work from other Visma offices or remotely from anywhere in Finland. However, we ho

pe to see you visit your team mates occasionally in our Helsinki office (Pasila, Mall of Tripla).
Our recruitment process includes 2 interview rounds and we contact all candidates latest within a week after the application period has ended. As this is a new position, the salary range depends on your relevant experience and seniority, but we are happy to hear your expectations.
Any questions?
Please contact Sr. Manager Olli Suihkonen at
olli.suihkonen@visma.com
.
More than 500 top experts at Visma Solutions help our customers to improve and automate their business. Our products for financial administration, payroll, operational management, and electronic signature are market leaders in their respective fields. We are part of the agile Nordic software group Visma, which employs 1 500 experts in Finland. Read more at
https://www.visma.fi/yritykset/visma-solutions-oy
.
Visma is a leading provider of mission-critical business software, with revenue of € 2.8 billion in 2025 and more than 2.4 million customers across Europe and Latin America. By simplifying and automating the work of SMBs and the public sector, we help unleash the power of digitalization and AI across our societies and economies.
Think of Visma as an ecosystem of companies, cultures, and people, united by shared values and the vision to
shape the future of society through technology
. In Finland we already have 1450+ employees and serve more than 200 000 customers.
Visit us at
visma.fi

jobcontent:
Simucube is a game-changer in the world of sim racing and simulators. At the heart of Hervanta, Tampere, our skilled and warm-hearted team of 65 professionals creates products known for their top performance and high quality.
With exciting projects underway and our business growing fast, we are now looking for a new colleague to join us as
IT Manager.
This is not a maintenance role. This is a foundation role.
You will build, structure, and take full ownership of Simucube’s IT function, ensuring our systems, tools, and ways of working support both today’s operations and tomorrow’s growth.
You’ll report directly to our CEO and work closely with teams across the organization. In a company of our size, IT is not a back-office function, it’s a business enabler, meaning your work will be both visible and meaningful from day one.
The role in a nutshell
This is a full-time, permanent position based in Hervanta, Tampere. While we offer flexible working arrangements, we value working together on-site, and we expect this role to have a regular presence at the office.
The salary range is 4000-5000€/month, depending on your experience.
This recruitment process is anonymous (read more below).
This is a new role at Simucube. Until now, IT responsibilities have been distributed across different people and partners. Your mission is to bring structure, ownership, and long-term thinking into our IT environment.
You will establish and lead our IT function, combining strategic thinking with hands-on sysadmin expertise. We currently work with our external IT support partner for day-to-day helpdesk operations, but how that partnership evolves is up to you.
You’ll work closely with leadership, finance, R&D, and operations, acting as an internal consultant who ensures IT enables business success.
Your responsibilities will include:
• Establishing and owning Simucube's IT function, building the structure, documentation, and processes we currently lack.
• Taking full ownership of our Microsoft environment (M365, Azure), identity and access management, and device lifecycle
• Leading collaboration with our external IT partner and ensuring smooth day-to-day operations
• Strengthening IT security and continuity (MFA, backups, risk management, incident response practices)
• Driving practical AI enablement and contributing to ERP development from an architecture and integration perspective
We believe this role will suit you if you:
• Have experience building or significantly developing IT environments in a growing organization
• Combine strategic thinking with hands-on technical capability
• Have strong expertise in Microsoft modern workplace environments and identity management
• Understand how modern IT environments fit together: SaaS, networking and security included
• Are comfortable leading external IT vendors and bringing structure into organically grown environments
• Are curious about AI tools and enjoy supporting people across the organization
This role requires someone who enjoys ownership. You are not stepping into a large IT department, you are building one. As Simucube and IT function grow, there may be opportunities to step into a broader leadership role over time.
In return, we offer you:
People-first culture:
We care about your well-being. That’s why we encourage regular breaks during the workday and offer a paid 30-minute mid-day stroll to recharge. You’ll also enjoy a €400/year well-being allowance (covering sports, culture, and wellness), extensive occupational healthcare, and access to high-quality sports equipment for borrowing.
Real ownership:
This is a rare opportunity to build an IT function from scratch in a fast-growing technology company.
Flexibility and trust:
We trust you to manage your time. Enjoy flexible working hours and hybrid work options. You’ll have access to top-notch tools and software, and yes, you’re welcome to use our equipment (including 3D printers) for your own hobby or DIY projects.
Continuous learning:
We support your growth. From training programs to literature of your choice, we want every team member to keep developing their skills and stay curious – especially in emerging areas like AI and automation.
About us
As a technology leader in sim racing, we are here to create long-lasting high-quality products, exceed customer expectations, and innovate so that racers can have the best gear available. Our products are used not only by simulation enthusiasts and dedicated e-sports athletes but also by some of the world’s fastest motorsport drivers to train off track.
While our products combine the precision of e-sports with industrial expertise and raw performance, the atmosphere at our office in Hervanta is cozy and welcoming. Our relaxed and flat organizational culture is symbolized by woolen socks, worn by everyone — including the CEO. This unique blend of cutting-edge technology and a supportive

work environment has earned us the title of Finland’s Most Human-Centric Workplace in 2024, by Hälsa.
Simucube (Granite Devices Oy) was founded in 2007 and is fully owned by its employees. In 2024, our revenue was over 15 M€.
Interested in joining us?
We are excited to hear from you! We review the applications as they arrive and may interview potential candidates already during the application period, so send your application as soon as possible, at the latest, on
Wednesday 13th of May 2026
.
The interview process will take place during May and early June and will consist of two rounds, giving you the opportunity to meet team members and gain additional insights into our work and culture.
Have questions about the role or what it’s like to work with us?
Reach out to Titta Saarinen, our Talent Acquisition Manager, at
titta.saarinen@simucube.com
or +358 45 807 2044.
Please note that this is an in-house role, and we are not seeking recruitment agency support at this time.
Candidates must be eligible to work in Finland, as we currently do not provide relocation or visa support.
Anonymous recruitment
As equal opportunities for everyone and continuous development of our ways of working are at the core of our company, we have started anonymous recruitment. We welcome people from different backgrounds and different life situations and are committed to promoting equal treatment of candidates.
Our goal with anonymous recruitment is to allow an increasingly diverse group of candidates to enter the interview stage and to select the most suitable person, regardless of their characteristics. By using anonymous recruitment, we can avoid unconscious biases affecting the outcome of the recruitment.
Anonymous recruitment at Simucube means that all applications are reviewed in anonymous mode in our recruitment system. The system will automatically blur personal information such as name, email address, and photo from both the application form and the CV. Based on the other information provided in the CV and application form, we select the most suitable candidates for the interview process. At this stage, all information provided by the candidate will be visible.
You can read more about our recruitment process at simucube.com/careers!

4 000–5 000 €/kk

Ilmianna työpaikkailmoitus

jobcontent:
We want you to shine!
Would you like to build a leading SaaS product? Come join us in developing Netvisor, top notch financial management software in Finland!
We are now looking for a Senior Software Developer to join our team working on
Netvisor Payroll
.
Netvisor
serves over 40 000 clients in Finland. This means that over 200 000 payslips are created and over 1 million invoices are sent monthly. Our product streamlines the daily lives of Finnish entrepreneurs, accountants, and employees by automating many important processes in invoicing, payroll, and expense management.
What to expect when you join us?
As a Senior Software Developer, you will be an integral part of a team dedicated to maintaining our existing software and developing new features. This team currently consists of six developers, supported by designers and architects. Your daily activities include full stack software development with modern (AI) tools and agile working methods, building innovative solutions and refining current functionality based on direct customer feedback.
In addition to AI assisted coding, you will be proactively identifying and driving improvements in the codebase, architecture, delivery, and ways of working. Product Owner will steer the planning and prioritization of the team's work from a technical perspective. You will have the opportunity to define your own personal OKRs that align with the team's goals, giving you a clear path for professional growth and impact.
Our company values are
entrepreneurial, responsible, dedicated, and inclusive
, and the people that thrive with us share these values. We also value responsibility and freedom, and believe in genuine teamplay. Our ways of working are intentionally fluid. We're currently using Scrum but actively reshaping our development lifecycle through AI, and we aren't afraid to ditch old habits for smarter ones. Join us if you want to help define how modern engineering teams should actually function.
What are we looking for?
In order to succeed in this role, you need a proven track record of building and shipping full stack projects into production and extensive experience in the following areas:
AI Mastery:
Expert-level proficiency in AI-assisted development tools and agentic coding. Proven ability to use AI to modernize, refactor, and maintain legacy codebases efficiently. Capability to provide internal training and establish best practices for AI-assisted coding within the dev team.
Building Customer-Facing AI Features:
Practical exposure to AI specification driven development and building product features that utilize AI technologies. Previous experience in designing and implementing AI-driven features (e.g. LLM-based platforms) into commercial software to improve end-user business processes.
Software Engineering:
Senior-level expertise in .NET (4.8) and ASP.NET. Strong proficiency in SQL Server and competent skills in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Leadership & Execution:
Seniority and ability to coach, mentor, and support more junior team members. Excellent problem-solving skills with the ability to work on complex technical issues. High level of operational efficiency and proactive attitude - you want to get things done efficiently and effectively.
Collaboration & Communication:
Experience in working with different stakeholders and a team-oriented mindset. Although English is our company language, in this role fluent communication skills in Finnish are required in order to be able to work with Finnish customer base and payroll related terminology.
Previous experience in Payroll or Financial Management software development or from a highly regulated industry is beneficial, but not at all necessary.
We want you to grow and thrive with us!
We are a committed, curious, and solution-oriented collective of team players. You'll have the autonomy and responsibility to drive our shared goals, backed by the mentorship of your leaders and the collective expertise of Visma's vast developer community. In addition to product development, we invest heavily in the development of our people. We provide opportunities for professional growth, learning new skills, and advancing your career. We have the opportunity to use 10% of our working hours to learn new things. With us, you'll undoubtedly build your own growth story. We want you to shine!
At Visma Solutions you'll have a cosy and relaxed work environment with over 500 colleagues, modern tools, and comprehensive
employee benefits
that support work-life balance. Visma Solutions Oy has been awarded the Hyvän mielen työpaikka® symbol as a recognition of commitment to strengthening the mental health of employees. The multinational corporation combined with the agile approach of Visma family companies creates a unique work environment and excellent career opportunities.
Apply now, and we're happy to tell you more!
Please send your applica

tion
latest on May 4th, 2026.
Visma Solutions has offices in Helsinki and Lappeenranta, but you can also work from other Visma offices or remotely from anywhere in Finland. However, it would be awesome if you occasionally visit your team mates in our offices.
We believe in diversity so we welcome applications from people with various backgrounds. Unfortunately we are not able to offer visa sponsorship or relocation services, so you would need to be already based in Finland and have a valid work permit.
The salary range is negotiable depending on your seniority and prior experience, but we are happy to hear your expectations. In addition to salary, we offer a wide range of benefits (including sports & culture, massage, dental, extended health care, recreation budget, rewards and bonuses, personnel fund, company parties, snacks in the office, etc). salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.

business ICT systems
take care of project metrics
agile project management
project management principles
project configuration management
project management
create project specifications
manage project information
perform project management
jobcontent:
CDO Palvelut Oy is a consulting company that offers high-level and independent expert services related to enterprise resource planning systems for both business management, financial management and information management. From us, you can get experienced information system and business experts to help with both large and small digitization needs. We have years of experience in the successful implementation of enterprise resource planning projects and have helped many organizations transition to the modern digital world of the 2020s. We dare to tackle even difficult situations and we also know how to steer crisis projects onto the right track and to the finish line successfully. We have strong references and experience in the processes of the energy sector, service business, manufacturing industry, construction and the ICT sector, among others. Our operations are focused on the Finnish market, but some of our customers operate on international markets.

Read more about us and our services: www.cdopalvelut.fi

Are you an expert with experience in ERP implementation or development projects who would like to work in the customer interface independently of the software? We have a key player role available and we are looking forward to you joining our team.

ERP consultant

What kind of things does your job description consist of? As our expert, you get to work in cooperation with interesting customer companies as well as different system suppliers. Genuine software and system independence enables you to search together with our customers for the most suitable solutions. Our goal is that you will be able to work with our entire service offering, taking into account your skills. Typically, it means operational control-related current state analyses, selection and bidding services, and project management services. The duration of projects is mostly several months, but projects lasting more than a year are no exception.

What are your daily tasks like? As is typical for project work, your work begins with a careful mapping of customer needs and more detailed definitions together with the customer. In the definition phase, goals and schedules for the project are created and project participants are selected. Larger projects also have a steering group, whose work we actively participate in. The actual project phase includes workshops, planning, stakeholder group work, communication and information, documentation, bidding, preparation for software implementations and actual implementations. Successful projects are also concluded competently and ensuring the satisfaction of stakeholders. Most of the time, you are responsible for pulling projects.

Within these sets of tasks, you make sure that the projects are implemented successfully, information moves, things progress and the goals are also reached in the project, without forgetting the appropriate documentation. Naturally, the set of tasks varies in our different services and also per project. You work independently, but not alone. Our entire team is here to support you.

What kind of expectations do we have for your skills? We are looking for a consulting and customer service-minded team player with a suitable educational background, sufficient experience in enterprise resource planning solutions - for example Microsoft Dynamics 365, IFS Cloud, SAP, Lemonsoft, etc., and the necessary process and technology expertise. You have the ability to plan and manage projects and the necessary change, as well as to prioritize the necessary practical measures. Smooth communication and documentation in both Finnish and English is important. The key is that you are able to offer our services in a customer-oriented, value-producing way and in plain language. We are interested in hearing openly about your background, so introduce us to your skills at a low threshold, even if you do not fulfill our wishes in all respects.

In terms of your working methods, you are independent, self-initiated and service-oriented. You react actively and quickly to the customer's needs and strive to solve challenges in a cooperative manner, seeing things through to the end.

We offer you the opportunity to develop your own skills and responsibility in our organization, a competitive salary that rewards results, and the support of a comfortable working community in the modern Tulli Business Park in Tampere. You get to influence the content of your work yourself and you can flexibly work part of the work week also remotely. We also offer our employees a lunch allowance, additional services and insurance related to health and dental care, and we support sports and cultural activities.

Inquiries and application instructions:
We do not use application periods and we hope that you submit your application as soon as possible. For more information about the task, please contact our partner Mentek's consultant Jani Kallio on 050 529 2715 or jani.kallio@mentek.fi

Apply for a place via the link/button in our announcement. Please also state your initial salary expectations with your application. Welcome to the recruitment process!

Mentek Advisors is an expert company offering recruitment, direct search and coaching services that connects experts and companies and networks in an impressive way. We offer our services supported by modern technology throughout Finland. Mentek comes from the words success factor. Behind the success of companies and communities is always the factor of success, the person. When the factors of success are recruited into suitable and meaningful work tasks, into mutually complementary work communities and into an encouraging management circle, success stories emerge. At Mentek, our mission is to enable success by helping success factors and companies find each other and unite their goals into a common future. We are also supportive in situations of change, when an individual or company faces challenges. www.mentek.fi

Apply for a place via the link/button in our announcement. Please also state your initial salary expectations with your application. Welcome to the recruitment process!

title: Security Engineer, Netvisor
job_responsibilities: Cross-Team Security Support: Acting as a high-level consultant and enabler for development teams, assisting them in integrating security best practices into both product features and infrastructure designs.

Architectural & Code Reviews: Reviewing and vetting infrastructure blueprints and application code solutions across our diverse platforms to ensure that they are secure-by-design and resilient against modern threats.

Infrastructure Collaboration: Working alongside our dedicated infrastructure team to support the management of our cloud security posture, providing expert guidance on cloud-native hardening and monitoring.

Vulnerability Management & Triage: Managing and interpreting results from CSPM and other scanning solutions, providing engineering teams with actionable, prioritized remediation paths that reduce friction.
Diverse Platform Adaptation: Implementing and adapting security controls fit for various solutions - understanding the nuances of securing both legacy monoliths and modern, event-driven applications.
Security Automation: Helping teams tune and manage security tooling (SAST, DAST, SCA) within their CI/CD pipelines to facilitate seamless "security as code" workflows.

Future-Tech Readiness: Staying on the pulse of emerging trends, such as agentic AI development, ensuring that our security boundaries for autonomous processes remain robust while our ecosystem evolves.
Senior Technical Expertise: You have a strong background in security engineering and are capable of reading and reviewing code across a diverse stack.
Cloud Infrastructure Fluency: You are highly proficient with cloud security principles and IaC (Infrastructure as Code), with a track record of supporting teams in maintaining a hardened environment.
Versatile Mindset: You can navigate the security requirements of different architectural patterns, from stable legacy systems to high-scale, distributed services.
Security Advocate: You are passionate about driving a security culture that goes beyond baseline compliance.
Enabler Mindset: You view security as a service to your fellow engineers, focusing on coaching and knowledge-sharing to elevate the collective performance of the development organization.
Fluent communication in English and Finnish: Although our company language is English, our Netvisor product is operating in the Finnish market.

jobcontent:
We want you to shine!
We are looking for an experienced (Senior)
Security Engineer
to join our Netvisor internal security team. In this role, you will work as part of an enabling team, dedicated to supporting our development and infrastructure units across the entire
Netvisor Product Development
.
The Netvisor product family is diverse, featuring everything from robust monolithic .NET foundations to modern, highly scalable, and event-driven services. Your goal is to empower the teams behind these services by providing architectural oversight, technical expertise, and tooling necessary to ensure our security posture consistently exceeds the requirements of the
Visma Application Security Program (VASP)
.
What can you expect when joining us?
As a Security Engineer, your role and responsibilities include:
Cross-Team Security Support:
Acting as a high-level consultant and enabler for development teams, assisting them in integrating security best practices into both product features and infrastructure designs.
Architectural & Code Reviews:
Reviewing and vetting infrastructure blueprints and application code solutions across our diverse platforms to ensure that they are secure-by-design and resilient against modern threats.
Infrastructure Collaboration:
Working alongside our dedicated infrastructure team to support the management of our cloud security posture, providing expert guidance on cloud-native hardening and monitoring.
Vulnerability Management & Triage:
Managing and interpreting results from CSPM and other scanning solutions, providing engineering teams with actionable, prioritized remediation paths that reduce friction.
Diverse Platform Adaptation:
Implementing and adapting security controls fit for various solutions - understanding the nuances of securing both legacy monoliths and modern, event-driven applications.
Security Automation:
Helping teams tune and manage security tooling (SAST, DAST, SCA) within their CI/CD pipelines to facilitate seamless "security as code" workflows.
Future-Tech Readiness:
Staying on the pulse of emerging trends, such as agentic AI development, ensuring that our security boundaries for autonomous processes remain robust while our ecosystem evolves.
What are we looking for?
Senior Technical Expertise:
You have a strong background in security engineering and are capable of reading and reviewing code across a diverse stack.
Cloud Infrastructure Fluency:
You are highly proficient with cloud security principles and IaC (Infrastructure as Code), with a track record of supporting teams in maintaining a hardened environment.
Versatile Mindset:
You can navigate the security requirements of different architectural patterns, from stable legacy systems to high-scale, distributed services.
Security Advocate:
You are passionate about driving a security culture that goes beyond baseline compliance.
Enabler Mindset:
You view security as a service to your fellow engineers, focusing on coaching and knowledge-sharing to elevate the collective performance of the development organization.
Fluent communication in English and Finnish:
Although our company language is English, our Netvisor product is operating in the Finnish market.
What do we offer?
We are a committed, curious, and solution-oriented collective of team players. You'll have the autonomy and responsibility to drive our shared goals, backed by the mentorship of your leaders and the collective expertise of Visma's security community. We provide opportunities for professional growth, learning new skills, and advancing your career. We have the opportunity to use 10% of our working hours to learn new things. With us, you'll undoubtedly build your own growth story. We want you to shine!
At Visma Solutions you'll have a cosy and relaxed work environment with over 500 colleagues, modern tools, and comprehensive
employee benefits
that support work-life balance. Visma Solutions Oy has been awarded the Hyvän mielen työpaikka® symbol as a recognition of commitment to strengthening the mental health of employees. The multinational corporation combined with the agile approach of Visma family companies creates a unique work environment and excellent career opportunities.
Apply now, and we're happy to tell you more!
Please send your application latest on
April 30th, 2026.
Visma Solutions has offices in Helsinki and Lappeenranta, but you can also work remotely from anywhere in Finland. Although we're connected throughout the day, we don't physically sit close to each other, so we don't expect you to either. However, we would like you to occasionally visit your team mates in our offices.
We believe diversity is a richness so we would love to have applications from people with various backgrounds. However, we are not able to offer visa sponsorship or relocation services, so you would need to be already based in Finland and have a valid work permit.
The s

alary range is negotiable depending on your seniority and prior experience, but we are happy to hear your expectations. In addition to salary, we offer a wide range of benefits (including personnel fund, sports, culture, massage, dental, extended health care, recreation budget, rewards and bonuses, company parties, snacks in the office, etc).
For more information
please contact Architecture Lead Marko Komu,
marko.komu@visma.com
.

jobapply_link: mailto:teemu@vaiha.fi?subject=Application for the Job - Keittiön vuoromestari
salary_range: Mara tes + sopimuspalkka kokemuksen ja työn vaatimustason mukaan

jobcontent:
Vaiha Oy is a Mikkeliläinen family-owned restaurant business. Vaiha restaurants include Vaiha Asema at the Mikkeli railway station, Vaiha Elias in Mikkeli's know-how quarter in Kaukola, as well as the restaurant Pormestari and the summer terrace restaurant Pikku Pormestari in the courtyard of Mikkeli's town hall. We prefer ingredients from local producers in the lunch, order and seasonal menus. Catering is a growing part of our business.

Your workplace is primarily the restaurant Pormestari or Vaiha Elias, but depending on the situation, work is also done in other workplaces and in catering operations.

As a kitchen supervisor, you are responsible for maintaining the quality of the restaurant's kitchen and organizing kitchen work in your own shift in cooperation with other personnel. In order to be successful in this job, you should have an education in the field as well as previous experience as a front-line worker and strong knowledge of practical work. You must also have know-how in purchasing, loss and margin management. A good working atmosphere is important to us, so you lead your shift with your own positive energy and example.

We offer a versatile work environment and varied tasks, an extensive occupational health care service, and leisure accident insurance. Become part of a professional and developing team!

For more information about the job, please contact restaurateur Teemu Kaijanen on 040 8393292
Applications and CVs to toimisto@vaiha.fi

We are looking for a professional cook in Lahti

Are you looking for a stable and good job where you can develop your skills?

The employment is open-ended and full-time (112.5 h / 3 weeks). Work shifts are arranged according to the opening hours of the offices.

We expect from you:
- basic skills in the restaurant and catering industry
- skills for front-line positions
-fluent Finnish language skills
- hygiene passport 
-previous experience in front-line positions in the restaurant or trade industry (preferred)

We offer you flexible shift work and the opportunity to develop your skills and advance in your career. There is an encouraging and positive atmosphere in the work community, where teamwork and good customer service play a central role. The position offers the opportunity to work in a versatile and developing environment, where every day brings with it new situations and learning opportunities.

Were you interested?
Send your application as soon as possible, because we process applications and interview candidates already during the application period. Also attach your CV (preferably in PDF format). The position will be filled as soon as a suitable person is found.

Bolt.Works is a national personnel service company. We are a fair employer, and we will help you at all stages of your career. We have a good atmosphere, great colleagues and the best application in the industry, through which you can take care of matters related to your employment with a couple of clicks. We always pay a salary commensurate with the task, your work experience and skills, as well as a bonus for excellent work performance. Occupational health services belong to you from day one. We have many career options and paths to offer you. Our customers are Finland's top players from various industries: industry, trade, logistics, hotel and restaurant, construction, property maintenance and offices. As a member of HELA and RT, we always operate according to the common rules of the game. So join the Bolttian gang, and you're guaranteed to be in the best company!

title: Family worker
salary_range: YSOS tes
what_we_expect: helps families in crisis situations
determine the child's well-being
support service users in developing skills
monitor the health of the service user
maintain the trust of service users
assess the client's drug addictions
psychiatry
jobcontent:
The central tasks of a family worker are family work, enhanced family work, and professional support staff activities. The task also includes home service for families with children. The family worker can also act as a working partner in assessing the need for services. With the help of family work, the aim is to strengthen the family's resources, parenting and support the interaction within the family.

The work is mobile and work done in families' homes, operating environments and networks. The family worker's working hours can vary between 7 and 8 p.m. The work is mainly done on weekdays, but if the family situation so requires, individual work shifts can also be done on weekends. The place of work is mainly in Kittilä, but there may also be work elsewhere in the service area.

We require the applicant to:

- At least a degree in home care, nurse or social worker.
 
- Driver's license and the possibility to use your own car.
 
- Excellent Finnish language skills.
 

We value:

- Work experience in services for families with children.
 
- Good cooperation and interaction skills and the ability to work independently.
 
- Methodological expertise, e.g. children to talk, nepsy instructor, substance abuse and psychiatry expertise.
 

The terms of the service relationship are determined in accordance with YSOS: Tess. The position is subject to a 6-month trial period. Regarding the vaccination protection of personnel, we comply with the vaccination protection required by the Work and Communicable Diseases Act. The person selected for the position must present the criminal record extract required by the Act on investigating the criminal background of those working with children (504/2002).

Send your free-form application and CV to eeva.lohiniva@sovea.fi

More information: Eeva Lohiniva, tel. 0504123677

title: Local nurse Hearing Centre, Surgical Hospital
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
what_we_expect: distribute meals to patients
jobcontent:
Hello, you nurse! Are you looking for a versatile day job that combines guiding patients of different ages and their close circle, manual skills, independent reception work and working in a multi-professional team? This could be the perfect opportunity for you!

We are looking for a personal nurse for the Hearing Center for a fixed-term employment until 31 December 2026. Start of work according to the agreement.

You work as a doctor's partner and take care of other tasks of a nurse. Later on, you will have the opportunity to familiarize yourself with, for example, group guidance of patients, sampling of earpieces, repairs and maintenance work.

Above all, good communication skills are needed in the work, because the focus is guiding and counseling patients. In addition, we value manual skills, technical know-how and knowledge of the anatomy of the ear and the physiology of hearing. You will meet our patients of all ages and their families in different life situations.

Audiological nursing is a constantly developing field, and in this job you have good opportunities to influence the development of your own work and skills together with the hearing center's multiprofessional team. We value a positive attitude and a desire to learn new things. We invest in your orientation, because the job cannot be learned in weeks. We consider previous experience in reception activities an advantage.

Do you want to do your part to influence the functional capacity and quality of life of our hearing impaired patients? Take another look at Kuulotalo: https://www.terveyskyla.fi/kuulotalo/palvelut/hus-kuulokeskusku-palvelut

We start the interviews already during the search. Recruitment is carried out for video and live interviews. So submit your application as soon as possible.

The job-specific salary is €2716.25/month and possible experience and other bonuses.

At HUS, you are part of the most effective treatment.

Job eligibility conditions

Nursing degree or basic nursing degree.

We require vaccination protection in accordance with the Infectious Diseases Act from those working in customer and patient facilities, and a health report on pulmonary tuberculosis from those working in healthcare operational units.

Contact information

Department manager Henna Kaipainen, tel. 0504287658.

title: Caregivers for summer jobs, Esperi Hoivakoti Reikonlinna
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
what_we_expect: implements the basic elements of nursing
jobcontent:
Hello, you licensed nurse! Do you want to make your summer meaningful and take part in creating a good everyday life for the elderly? At Esperi Hoivakoti Reikonlinna, located in Urjala, we are looking for a home care nurse or a nurse for summer work from June 8 to August 9, 2026, with the possibility to continue after the summer. Read more about the position below and apply to us as a summer colleague!

Esperi Hoivakoti Reikonlinna as a summer workplace

In Hoivakoti Reikonlinna, we offer 24-hour service housing for the elderly and people with dementia, with 46 places for residents. We are located in a beautiful environment close to nature in Urjala, where we spend summer days together in a cozy yard. When working with our residents, the key is an appreciative encounter, which reinforces the relevance of the resident's experience. Many of our employees find the most meaningful moments when they can sit next to a resident for a while and reminisce about the life they lived. Daily life in Reikonlinna is brightened up by diverse hobby activities, friendly dogs, and singing lessons and quizzes organized by volunteers. Our common goal is the happiest residents of Finland!

Find out more about our everyday life on Instagram: [@esperi_reikonlinna](https://www.instagram.com/esperi_reikonlinna/)

What could your typical summer day look like with us?

As a nurse, you create a safe, meaningful and valuable everyday life for our residents with your presence. Your work includes e.g. basic care, supporting the ability to function, monitoring the well-being of our residents, going outside with our residents and various treatment measures. In addition, you participate in everyday arrangements and activities together with the rest of the team. Your job also includes medical care when you have completed your medical licenses. As you gain experience, you will have the opportunity to take on more responsibility and deepen your skills if you wish.

Summer employment information

The summer employment relationship is temporary for the period 8 June-9 August 2026, after which there may be an extension. We hope that you could start the orientation shifts in good time before the summer work. The work is hourly work (1115 h / 3 weeks) and 2-shift work, with an emphasis on evening and weekend shifts.

Salary

When hiring, we follow the private collective agreement in the social services sector. The position-specific base salary is, depending on the experience bonus:

- in the role of family nurse €2415.762612.33/month (salary category G20C)

- in the position of a nurse €2723.922969.00/month (salary category G23D)

What do we expect from you?

- you are ready to become a nurse or a nurse

- you have valid medical licenses, a hygiene passport and vaccination protection according to the Infectious Diseases Act

- you have fluent Finnish language skills

- you have empathy, the skill of meeting and a desire to learn new things

Previous experience working with the elderly is an advantage. We also ask to see a criminal record extract in accordance with the Criminal Records Act.

91% of summer employees would recommend us as a place to work

We invest in a good start and comfort for summer workers. By investing in your orientation, we ensure that your work is safe and that the start goes well. Last year, we collected feedback from our summer employees, and up to 91% of respondents would recommend Esper as a place to work!

During your summer employment, you can take advantage of our occupational health partner Mehiläinen, and through Cityshopper you will have access to comprehensive offers and discounts, e.g. restaurant and travel services. In addition, you will receive a summer job gift at the beginning of the employment relationship, if you are a new summer employee.

Would you see yourself as a summer employee with us?

Absolutely awesome! Submit your application by 17.5. by and tell us about yourself! We review applications already during the application period and agree on the start of work as soon as a suitable person is found for the position. Please note that for data security reasons we only process applications submitted through the recruitment system.

Did you have any questions? You can contact the recruiting supervisor: unit manager Soili Uusitalo (contacts weekdays 9-15), tel. 044 446 0300 // soili.uusitalo@esperi.fi

Esperi Hoivakoti Reikonlinna

Esperi is a Finnish national company offering housing services. We employ more than 7,200 social and health professionals, and Esperi homes can be found in more than 100 locations around Finland. Elderly people, people undergoing mental health rehabilitation and clients of the disability service live in our homes. We do care work in a resident-oriented and high-quality manner. High resident satisfaction (50 NPS) is a matter of our pride. Come and experience for yourself what it's like to work for us! You can find all our open positions [here](https://esperi.fi/tyopaikat/avoimet-tyopaikat/).

Phone: 044 446 0300 E-mail: soili.uusitalo@esperi.fi Location: Vanhainkodintie 6, Urjala

jobcontent:
ANNU social services is now looking for a home care worker to join us to create a better tomorrow for our wonderful customers.

The work of a caregiver at ANNU social services is versatile and rewarding. You work in the home service for families with children with our lovely customer families. Working hours vary according to the families' wishes and your working opportunities. A person who is able to work independently and professionally in customer work with families is successful in our work.

Your working day as a caregiver includes, for example, taking care of the basic needs of the child being cared for; taking care of hygiene and meals, playing, outdoor activities or supporting in hobbies. You may help the family, for example, with housekeeping or cooking. Please note that the job is not an occasional gig, but we are looking for employees who can commit to customer relationships lasting several months.

We offer flexible and meaningful paid work:

- You yourself influence your working hours and when and where you work. Increasing working hours is also possible.
 
- The work is well suited to be done alongside studies or another job. Working time is focused on daytime, but can also be afternoon/evening focused.

The work of a caregiver at ANNU social services is versatile and rewarding. You work in the home service for families with children with our lovely customer families. Working hours vary according to the families' wishes and your working opportunities. A person who is able to work independently and professionally in customer work with families is successful in our work.

Your working day as a caregiver includes, for example, taking care of the basic needs of the child being cared for; taking care of hygiene and meals, playing, outdoor activities or supporting in hobbies. You may help the family, for example, with housekeeping or cooking. Please note that the job is not an occasional gig, but we are looking for employees who can commit to customer relationships lasting several months.

We offer flexible and meaningful paid work:

- You yourself influence your working hours and when and where you work. Increasing working hours is also possible.
 
- The work is well suited to be done alongside studies or another job. Working time is focused on daytime, but can also be afternoon/evening focused.
 
- At ANNU social services, you have a supervisor who supports your work
 
- Depending on your choice, we pay the salary either once or twice a month.
 
- We offer a comprehensive orientation before starting the work tasks, the orientation is paid and it is paid with the first salary payment.
 
- We comply with the collective agreement of the private social sector and pay the salary for the work in accordance with the collective agreement (salary category G20C)
 

ANNU sosialiapalvelut is a company founded on Valentine's Day 2023, piloted by professionals in the social welfare sector. We offer services for families with children, young people and adults all over Finland!

Welcome to join us in creating the best workplace in the social sector in Finland! Please apply soon, we will fill the position when a suitable person is found! We use teams remote connection when necessary as part of our recruitment process.

More information: Ida Ehrman, ida@annu.fi, 041 319 1773 (weekdays 10:00-12:00)

Application link: https://form.hailer.com/cd1f832ac3525d7cf917a5bcce84be5f

salary_range: Yksityisen sosiaalipalvelualan TES

assists in the development of children's personal skills
disability care
infant care
deal with children's problems
help children with homework
workplace cleanliness
play with children
clean the rooms
prepare ready meals
give first aid
maintain relationships with the children's parents
support children's well-being
supervise children
act empathically
promote inclusion
promote social change
accept own responsibility
follow the instructions of the organization
listen actively
work in communities
adheres to socially just working principles
support the positivity of young people
can withstand stress
apply a person-centered treatment method

comply with quality standards in social services

jobcontent:
We are looking for CAREGIVERS for home service for families with children in the welfare area of Länsi-uusmaa, Kirkkonummi! In the home service for families with children, you take care of the children of our client families and you get to play and go out with the wonderful children. At the same time, you concretely support the smoothness of the families' everyday life and the parents' ability to cope with your own work, and thus help to prevent and ease the problems of children and families as early as possible.

We offer you work according to your own life situation, in families that suit you. As a rule, your work shift in the family lasts 2-4 hours at a time. Work is available from Monday to Friday mainly between 16:00 and 21:00 and on Saturdays between 08:00 and 18:00. You can choose when you want to work! It would be best if you could commit to at least one of our customer families for about 2-3 months. As a rule, the work takes place in our client families' own homes and around their homes in the Kirkkonummi area.

From us you get:

\-€16.36/h + evening and weekend supplements according to the TES of the private social service sector

\-Transition bonus for each transfer during your working day, if you work in several families on the same day.

\-Comprehensive, paid, orientation to your job

\-Opportunity for regular training and development of your work

\-Emergency first aid training

\-Your supervisor I will provide support alongside your independent work whenever you need it

\- Statutory occupational health care

\-Accident insurance

We also have a referral bonus. If you recommend Hoivanett as a workplace to a friend and the recommendation leads to recruitment, we will pay you a €50 referral bonus.

WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

\-You have a NURSING degree or another applicable social or health degree (child counselor, nanny, home care worker)

\-Professional experience working with children and families

\-Self-initiated attitude and ability to work independently

\-Good oral and written Finnish language skills / PLEASE, NOTE! In this position, good written and spoken skills in FINNISH are required in this position!

You must also have at least one reference from previous employment with children and a clean criminal record to work with children. The person selected for the position must present the employer with a criminal record extract in accordance with Section 6, subsection 2 of the Criminal Records Act. The position requires registration in Valvira's central register of professionals.

We also value:

\-Experience working with children in need of special support (e.g. nepsy challenges, autism spectrum)

SEND YOUR APPLICATION THROUGH THE FORM! :)

Sivina Hoivanet exists to make everyday life easier for families with children in various life situations. When you work for us, you help families and gain your own professional experience in a variety of ways. With additional training, we make sure that your skills stay up-to-date. Flexible gig work is suitable for many life situations, for example alongside another job, studies or part-time retirement. We belong to Sivina companies.

Send your application via the form!

salary_range: Tehtäväkohtainen palkka alkaen 2721,38 €/kk
what_we_expect: Come and make your summer meaningful - we have a warm-hearted team waiting for you, who support and encourage you every day.
