what_we_offer: We offer you a summer job where you can do meaningful work and develop your professional skills. You will get a good orientation and team support throughout the summer.
jobcontent:
Your assignment

As a caregiver, you work with home care clients in their own home. Your work is versatile and responsible: you plan, implement and evaluate treatment with a rehabilitative approach together with a multi-professional team. Your work environment is variable, so a driver's license and the use of your own car are necessary. We use the Pegasos OMNI and Pegasos Mukana operational control systems and the RAI evaluation system.

What we offer you

We offer you a summer job where you can do meaningful work and develop your professional skills. You will get a good orientation and team support throughout the summer. 

What we expect from you

You have a LOVe drug license or you are ready to complete it within the agreed deadline. Knowledge of the RAI system and good IT skills are considered an advantage. We value experience in care work and home care for the elderly, good communication skills and a positive attitude towards development. Good oral and written Finnish language skills are necessary to succeed in the job. We expect from you responsibility, flexibility and a genuine desire to meet customers individually and respectfully. Home care work is mobile, you need a driver's license and the ability to use your own car. In the teams in the central area, you can get by on foot or by bicycle.

Come and make your summer meaningful - we have a warm-hearted team waiting for you, who support and encourage you every day.

Submit your application and join us in building a better everyday life!
We interview applicants already during the application period and fill the positions as soon as suitable people are found - so don't wait, apply now.

The eligibility requirement for the position is a professional basic degree in social and health care (nursing) or a previous school-level degree in the field and Valvira registration.

Students are also considered. In order to work for us as a home nurse, you must have completed medical care studies and have 120-179 competence points from the home care studies or 90-139 credits from the nursing studies. If you have completed 60-119 nursing competence points, you can work for us as a care assistant, apply for a summer job search for a care assistant.

We are the workplace of approximately 23,000 experts and one of the largest employers in our country. We make sure that all half a million people in our region get the social and health services they need, and we secure rescue operations. We work on important issues that affect everyone!

We are #do-gooders!

title: Substitute caregiver for elderly people in Hauho's Koivukoto
jobcontent:
Our work community is looking for a new nursing professional to join us in doing valuable work with the elderly. We are looking for a local nurse for Koivukoto Hauho for a substitute position, which will last until February 16, 2027. You can start the job on May 25. according to the agreement. 

At Koivukodo, we offer round-the-clock long-term service housing for our 42 residents. Our house is divided into five small homes, which we call nests. We have Metso-Teerenpesä, Peipon- and Pääskynpesä, and Kiurun- and Tilhenpesä. In Peipon- and Pääskynpes, we focus on treating residents with memory problems. Each nest has its own nest keepers, but if necessary, we move flexibly between nests. Your own home base is waiting for you too. All nests are located in a terraced house on the same level. 

You get support for your work from the nurse team coordinator and a nurse, from home nurses, from a care assistant, and from your local manager. We work in three shifts. When planning work shifts, you can influence your own work shifts. 

As a local nurse, your duties include taking care of the residents' daily care, hygiene and medical treatment. You also act as a personal caregiver for the clients assigned to you. As a personal caregiver, your work includes care and preparation of service plans in cooperation with the resident and relatives, and RAI functional ability assessments when agreed. You also act as an information broker and contact person between the resident's relatives, guardian, doctor and other important stakeholders. At Koivukodo, residents are met as individuals, and your duties include taking care of the residents' comfort by organizing active activities. 

We expect a positive attitude and good teamwork skills from you. We are looking for a colleague who is interested in working as part of our team and wants to nurture the positive atmosphere of our unit with us. The eligibility requirement for the position is a basic degree in social and health care or another equivalent degree. Previous experience working with the elderly is to your advantage, but the most important thing is a genuine interest in the well-being of the elderly and a rehabilitative way of working. We require registration in the central register of social and healthcare professionals before the start of the employment relationship. Participating in medical treatment requires a valid medical treatment permit, which you can update if necessary or complete with us at the beginning of the employment relationship. 

We offer you a responsible position in a positive work environment.-We invest together in well-being at work. At us, professional competence is developed by participating in the trainings offered by Oma Häme. New ideas and operational development are welcome things in our work community. 

At Oma Häme, you also have access to a wide range of employment benefits: 
- Lunch allowance 
- Commuting benefit 
- Cultural, well-being and exercise benefit Epassi Flex 
- Occupational health at Terveystalo 
- Comprehensive training offer 

Want to join us? Submit your application no later than May 14, 2026. by 3:00 p.m. The application must be submitted through the Kuntarekry system. We start recruitment discussions already during the application period, and the interviews will be held no later than week 21. 

The address of the workplace is Kurrenkuja 1, 14700 Hauho 

The job-specific salary is €2647.17/month. In addition to the task-specific salary, we pay more based on experience, which can be 3% or 8%. The accumulation of vacation days is also determined based on the experience bonus. The working time is 38.25 hours per week. 

A person working with the elderly must submit a criminal record extract referred to in § 6.4 of the Criminal Records Act in accordance with § 28.1 of the Social and Health Care Supervision Act (741/2023), which must be submitted before the start of the employment relationship. 

Salary and other terms of employment are determined according to the SOTE agreement. There is a trial period. In direct patient and customer work, the vaccination protection required by Section 48 of the Infectious Diseases Act and the report on respiratory tuberculosis in accordance with Section 55 are required. At Oma Hämee, we want to support scientific research work by granting paid time off, for example, to write a dissertation, to do the final work of a higher university degree or to do other scientific research. In accordance with our strategy, we promote the diversity and equality of our personnel.

Oma Häme invites you to join us in building a meaningful tomorrow together! According to the survey, 70% of our employees would recommend us as an employer to a friend. (Source: TTL). We are looking for dedicated employees who share our values ​​and passion for equality, customer orientation, effectiveness and courage. Why Oma Häme? With us, you have the opportunity to directly influence the well-being of our customers. We work as a community towards an equal service, involving customers and employees in planning and development. We use science-based methods that bring changes to our customers' lives.

The premises of the new Assi hospital will be available to us in early 2026. Oma Häme is responsible for the well-being of 171,000 residents in 11 municipalities with 7,100 employees.

Executive director of housing services for the elderly 
Wirva-Tuulia Nykänen, 
phone 044-5694633, 
wirva-tuulia.nykanen@omahame.fi

title: Caregivers for gigs, Esperi Hoivakoti Merivuokko
salary_range: Palkkauksessa noudatamme yksityistä sosiaalipalvelualan työehtosopimusta. Tehtäväkohtainen pohjapalkka on kokemuslisistä riippuen 14,82-16,03 €/h (palkkaluokka G20C).
what_we_expect: implements the basic elements of nursing
jobcontent:
Hello paramedic or nurse! Are you looking for occasional gig work in Uusitakaupunki? We at Hoivakoti Merivuoko are looking for gig workers read more and apply easily!

Hoivakoti Merivuokko as a workplace

Hoivakoti Merivuokko offers a round-the-clock housing service for the elderly in Uusikaupunki. The safe and warm home has a total of 30 places for residents. The daily life of the residents includes versatile activities. Interesting visitors, musical performances, chair jumping moments and joint parties bring joy to the daily life of both residents and caregivers. The goal is to maintain the resident's existing resources for as long as possible. The home's peaceful and beautiful yard area creates a pleasant setting for outdoor activities or enjoying coffee moments. Hoivakoti is located in Uussakaupunki, near the seaside in the green residential area of ​​Janhua. There are good bicycle paths in the area, and the market is easily accessible on foot or by car for 1 km.

What is the dream day of Simon, who lives in Merivuoko, made of? Go watch the video [here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=otBqfRF6s0k).

Your working day as a nurse with us

We familiarize the gig workers with our unit and our work practices, so that it's nice to come to work, and you know what is expected of you. Working should feel safe and you can also trust that you can always ask your colleagues for help!

Your working day consists of caring for and guiding the residents, planning and participating in the overall care of the residents, and activity activities. If you have completed the medical treatment licenses, your duties may also include the implementation of medical treatment for the residents. The most important thing for us is that you know how to deal with elderly people with respect and do nursing work according to their ability to function. It is an advantage for us if you have already graduated as a nursing professional and you can also complete the medical licenses in our unit, but we also welcome competent students!

Employment information

The work is shift work. We hope to start the gigs as soon as possible. Gig work is based on a contract that does not obligate you to any specific number of hours. You can start gigging with us right away and continue gigging for as long as it suits you.

Salary

When hiring, we follow the private collective agreement in the social services sector. The job-specific base salary is €14.82-16.03/h depending on the experience bonus (salary category G20C).

Gig workers are paid an hourly wage and the salary is paid twice a month. Any shift-specific bonuses come on top of the basic salary.

What do we expect from you?

- A brisk and self-initiated attitude

- Nursing or nursing degree or sufficiently completed studies

- Vaccination protection and hygiene passport according to the Infectious Diseases Act, valid medical licenses are an advantage

- Fluent Finnish language skills (at least B1 level) so that you can communicate with our residents and colleagues, work on the phone and make resident registrations

Why should you come to work for us?

Flexible work. Work as much as you like.

A warm welcome. We warmly welcome you as part of our team. We ensure you a good orientation and you get the support of the entire work community for your work. We work together as a team for the benefit of our residents, our goal is quality caring and well-being personnel.

Learning something new. As a work environment, our home is interesting and versatile. We want to provide you with good conditions to carry out your work and develop your skills.

How does it sound? Apply for us as a colleague!

Submit your application by 15.5. by! Please act as soon as possible, because we review applications already during the application period and agree on the start of a new job as soon as suitable people are found for the position.

Do you want to hear more about the assignment? For more information, please contact unit manager Jaana Kiesi on 044 720 2437, ext. jaana.kiesi@esperi.fi.

It would be a pleasure to meet you!

Esperi Hoivakoti Merivuokko

Esperi is a Finnish national company offering housing services. We employ more than 7,200 social and health professionals, and Esperi homes can be found in more than 100 locations around Finland. Elderly people, people undergoing mental health rehabilitation and clients of the disability service live in our homes. We do care work in a resident-oriented and high-quality manner. High resident satisfaction (50 NPS) is a matter of our pride. Come and experience for yourself what it's like to work for us! You can find all our open positions [here](https://esperi.fi/tyopaikat/avoimet-tyopaikat/).

Phone: 044 720 2437 Email: jaana.kiesi@esperi.fi Location: Kaljuunakuja 5

Caregivers for gigs, Esperi Hoivakoti Merivuokko

Hello paramedic or nurse! Are you looking for occasional gig work in Uusitakaupunki? We at Hoivakoti Merivuoko are looking for gig workers read more and apply easily!

Hoivakoti Merivuokko as a workplace

Hoivakoti Merivuokko offers a round-the-clock housing service for the elderly in Uusikaupunki. The safe and warm home has a total of 30 places for residents. The daily life of the residents includes versatile activities. Interesting visitors, musical performances, chair jumping moments and joint parties bring joy to the daily life of both residents and caregivers. The goal is to maintain the resident's existing resources for as long as possible. The home's peaceful and beautiful yard area creates a pleasant setting for outdoor activities or enjoying coffee moments. Hoivakoti is located in Uussakaupunki, near the seaside in the green residential area of ​​Janhua. There are good bicycle paths in the area, and the market is easily accessible on foot or by car for 1 km.

What is the dream day of Simon, who lives in Merivuoko, made of? Go watch the video [here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=otBqfRF6s0k).

Your working day as a nurse with us

We familiarize the gig workers with our unit and our work practices, so that it's nice to come to work, and you know what is expected of you. Working should feel safe and you can also trust that you can always ask your colleagues for help!

Your working day consists of caring for and guiding the residents, planning and participating in the overall care of the residents, and activity activities. If you have completed the medical treatment licenses, your duties may also include the implementation of medical treatment for the residents. The most important thing for us is that you know how to deal with elderly people with respect and do nursing work according to their ability to function. It is an advantage for us if you have already graduated as a nursing professional and you can also complete the medical licenses in our unit, but we also welcome competent students!

Employment information

The work is shift work. We hope to start the gigs as soon as possible. Gig work is based on a contract that does not obligate you to any specific number of hours. You can start gigging with us right away and continue gigging for as long as it suits you.

Salary

When hiring, we follow the private collective agreement in the social services sector. The job-specific base salary is €14.82-16.03/h depending on the experience bonus (salary category G20C).

Gig workers are paid an hourly wage and the salary is paid twice a month. Any shift-specific bonuses come on top of the basic salary.

What do we expect from you?

- A brisk and self-initiated attitude

- Nursing or nursing degree or sufficiently completed studies

- Vaccination protection and hygiene passport according to the Infectious Diseases Act, valid medical licenses are an advantage

- Fluent Finnish language skills (at least B1 level) so that you can communicate with our residents and colleagues, work on the phone and make resident registrations

Why should you come to work for us?

Flexible work. Work as much as you like.

A warm welcome. We warmly welcome you as part of our team. We ensure you a good orientation and you get the support of the entire work community for your work. We work together as a team for the benefit of our residents, our goal is quality caring and well-being personnel.

Learning something new. As a work environment, our home is interesting and versatile. We want to provide you with good conditions to carry out your work and develop your skills.

How does it sound? Apply for us as a colleague!

Submit your application by 15.5. by! Please act as soon as possible, because we review applications already during the application period and agree on the start of a new job as soon as suitable people are found for the position.

Do you want to hear more about the assignment? For more information, please contact unit manager Jaana Kiesi on 044 720 2437, ext. jaana.kiesi@esperi.fi.

It would be a pleasure to meet you!
